evidence_basis: repeated-scan CV growth with increasing peak current per cycle
recommendation: Use as baseline MeCN/TBA/Pt positive after source spot-check.

evidence_basis: film cycled repeatedly between oxidized and neutral states after deposition
recommendation: Use as baseline heteroaromatic positive.

evidence_basis: film redox response with E0 around +480 mV
recommendation: Use as N-substituted pyrrole positive; source-check exact locator.

evidence_basis: voltammetric and potentiostatic growth of thick insoluble electroactive film
recommendation: Strong matched positive for dialkoxy-thiophene chemistry.

evidence_basis: CV electrodeposition followed by redox response of deposited film
recommendation: Use as selenophene/dioxy positive with exact source review.

evidence_basis: potentiodynamic film via radical-cation dimerization then growth
recommendation: Use with matched carbazole negatives from same paper.

evidence_basis: fast radical-cation dimerization kdim about 1.5e7 M-1 s-1 gives thick adherent film
recommendation: Best matched carbazole YES paired with tert-butyl and disubstituted NO labels.

evidence_basis: pulse-nucleated electrodeposition plus CV growth
recommendation: Useful positive but mixed solvent should be excluded from exact-baseline metric or stratified separately.

evidence_basis: degradation and ring opening at high potential gives low-quality non-conjugated polyfuran
recommendation: Use as standard-medium furan negative with caveat that statement is review-framed.

evidence_basis: slow radical-cation dimerization kdim <= 1.5e6 M-1 s-1 and no efficient electropolymerization
recommendation: High-value matched NO in same condition family as 3-ethylcarbazole YES.

evidence_basis: kdim <= 1.5e6 M-1 s-1 under 1.4 V/SCE and no efficient electropolymerization
recommendation: Strong blocked-growth carbazole negative.

evidence_basis: tert-butyl groups at 3 and 6 suppress radical-cation coupling and no efficient electropolymerization occurs
recommendation: Best structural carbazole NO for coupling-position sterics.

evidence_basis: no efficient electropolymerization under 1.4 V/SCE; higher potentials create uncontrolled phenyl-centered radicals
recommendation: Use only with the stated potential-window condition.

evidence_basis: same no-efficient-electropolymerization regime as 3-phenylcarbazole under controlled window
recommendation: Use as potential-window-specific NO; avoid generalizing to harsher potentials.

evidence_basis: radical cation dimerizes to tetraphenylbenzidine rather than propagating to film
recommendation: Use as arylamine dimer-only negative; stratify mixed-solvent condition.

evidence_basis: aniline did not polymerize on platinum at low concentration although films form on GC/CF
recommendation: Use as medium/electrode-specific NO not as universal aniline negative.

evidence_basis: water quenches radical cation and Eox exceeds water decomposition potential
recommendation: Source DOI should be resolver-checked before headline validation use.

evidence_basis: oxidation peak present but polymer product remains soluble so no insoluble film forms
recommendation: High-value soluble-product negative; recover exact SMILES before metric use.

evidence_basis: single oxidation peak decays per cycle with no reduction or film growth
recommendation: Use as wrong-solvent NO; keep separate from MeCN terthiophene YES.

evidence_basis: alpha positions are blocked so normal alpha-alpha electropolymerization is impossible
source_locator: J. Electroanal. Chem. 2009 PII S0022072809002332 citing earlier work
recommendation: Mechanism-based tier-B negative; source DOI/locator must be pinned before promotion.
